$comment: Hard CI LOC limits per module — dev-docs/design/01-architecture.md §11. Counted like the reference baseline: all .ts incl. comments & blanks, *.test.ts / *.spec.ts excluded. `total` is the derived sum of the budgets (asserted by scripts/loc-budget.mjs); never hand-edit it as a gate. Editing any per-module number requires a rationale line in the same PR (§11 speed bump).
$rationale_views: 2026-06-14 (M7): views raised 2500→4000 (§11 speed-bump). The architecture §11 estimate of 2500 predated implementation and is infeasible for the realized M7 scope — six series renderers coded verbatim from study-06 §4.5–§4.11 (incl. candlestick 3-pass §4.10 + histogram column-geometry cache §4.11), shared line-base/bar-base, the PaneScene registry, the SeriesKind engine + decimate helper, four scene-source services (grid/crosshair/price-line/last-value-label), the ranked hit-test service, and axis-layout. Realized ≈3834 LOC; code-only (comments+blanks stripped) ≈2600 already exceeds 2500, so no comment-trimming can fit it without violating the spec-of-record citation discipline. ≈4000 reflects the natural size; M11 (parity hardening, which spans views) may warrant a further documented bump. Total recomputed 17950→19450.
$rationale_api: 2026-06-15 (M9): api raised 2200→3100 (§11 speed-bump). The architecture §11 estimate of 2200 predated implementation and is infeasible for the realized M9 scope — the FULL design-02 §3.2 public surface plus the join point, across 13 files: six handle/facade modules (chart §7, series §8, time-scale §9, price-scale §10, pane §11, price-line §11.1 — each a thin map-through over the model/host with its chart-owned Port + the §16.5 disposed-guard), the §5–§6 options boundary (the four §5.3 normalizations + the three-layer defaults pipeline + §4.3 snapshots), the §14 event hubs, the §16 error taxonomy, the §13.1/§13.2 series definitions + defineSeries + time guards, the §3.2 engine/gfx/seam re-exports, and create-chart.ts — the createChartWith JOIN POINT that constructs model (M5) + views scene (M7) + host (M8), wires the Timeline+PlotStore+SeriesKind→PaneScene data pipeline + the six §2 cached-handle ports + the §14 hubs, and is the sole backend-canvas importer (§3.1). Realized ≈2959 LOC; the seven facade/options/events/errors files alone are ≈1894 and are fixed public surface. ≈3100 reflects the natural size with modest M10–M14 headroom. Total recomputed 19450→20350.
$rationale_host_m10: 2026-06-15 (M10 ph1): host raised 2000→2050 (§11 speed-bump). The perf §9.6 instrumentation contract is homed in host: host/profiling.ts (FrameStats + IPerfSink, the per-frame snapshot the IPerfSink stream carries), the FrameProfiler bundle + the scheduler's reset-at-frame-entry / read-at-endFrame FrameStats assembly (one field per §9.6 lane), the SurfaceHost replayMs bracket, and the ChartHost profiler threading. All of it is __TV_PROFILE__-guarded and byte-identical-stripped from the shipped build (verified by scripts/profiling-free.mjs, §3.3.1), so it costs 0 shipped bytes — but it is real source that counts against the LOC gate. Realized ≈2009; 2050 leaves ~40 for the M10 host-side bench wiring follow-ups (inputLagFrames source, §4.4.9). Total recomputed 20350→20450.
$rationale_api_m10: 2026-06-15 (M10 ph1): api raised 3100→3150 (§11 speed-bump). create-chart.ts gained the perf §9.6 wiring: ChartEnvironment's perfSink?/frameCounters? fields, the per-chart FrameCounters + FrameProfiler construction, and threading the one accumulator into the timeline + each series' PlotStore/ItemBuffer via setCounters — all __TV_PROFILE__-guarded (0 shipped bytes, §3.3.1). Realized ≈3107; 3150 leaves a small M10–M14 margin. Total recomputed 20350→20450 (combined with $rationale_host_m10).
$rationale_views_m11: 2026-06-15 (M11 parity — decimation): views raised 4000→4250 (§11 speed-bump, anticipated by the M7 $rationale_views note). The §6.3 decimation conformance work adds src/views/series/decimate-conformance.fixture.ts — a HEADLESS, non-test fixture module (.fixture.ts is countable; only *.test.ts/*.spec.ts are excluded) that the decimate tests and the doc-03 §9 backend conformance suite both compose against, exactly as scripts/demo-chart.mjs composes against a stub ISurface. It carries the perf §4.1 mulberry32 seeded-walk store builder, the S8/R2 scene parameters (the ≤60-command cap of §4.4.3), the stub COLUMN RASTER (composeColumns + a connected-segment scan-converter), the per-row overlapping-1px reference envelope (referenceColumnEnvelope — the §11.1 'overdraw-elimination-only' baseline), and diffColumns — none of which can live in a *.test.ts without being unusable by the backend suite. The decimate.ts hardening itself (on-screen device-column clipping so 'one segment per device column' and columns ≤ deviceWidth hold under the EXTENDED window, §6.3/caps §4.2) added only a few lines. Realized views ≈4207; 4250 leaves ~40 headroom for the remaining M11 views parity streams. Total recomputed 20450→20700.
$rationale_api_m11: 2026-06-15 (M11 parity — INTEGRATE): api raised 3150→3250 (§11 speed-bump). The M11 integrate pass wires the deferred parity behaviors into create-chart.ts (the join point that owns the data pipeline + ports): (1) the M9 price-line RENDERING deferral is CLOSED — createPriceLine now registers a views createPriceLineSource (band AboveSeries) into the owning pane's scene with a provider that maps the stored price through the SAME per-frame PriceConverter the series rendered with (a captured lastPrice cell), removePriceLine + removeSeries unregister it (the disposeExtras hook on Wired), and the create/option/remove paths invalidate at Render (the line is a BASE-layer source, which an Overlay frame does not repaint); (2) the screenshot includeCrosshair flag is threaded api→host (ChartHostFacade.takeScreenshot(includeCrosshair) → ChartHost.takeScreenshot → captureScreenshot → backend composeSnapshot, §8.6); (3) the perf §4.4.9 inputLagFrames source is computed (a profile-only frame/input-seq tracker the gesture hooks mark via markInput) instead of the hardcoded ()=>0. Realized api ≈3183; 3250 leaves headroom for the remaining M11 api parity (animation/mode applyOptions wiring as the real time-scale geometry lands). Total recomputed 20700→20800.
$rationale_api_m12: 2026-06-15 (M12 — HOST PRIMITIVE BINDING): api raised 3250→3500 (§11 speed-bump). M12 closed the M8/M9 §9.1-slot-4 / §12 seam gap adversarial review exposed: create-chart.ts's attachPrimitive was inert (pane handle a no-op; model Series.attachPrimitive pushed to a list nobody read), so the four first-party plugins were inert in a live chart. The wiring adds (design 02 §12 + design 05 §2.2): (1) a per-chart createPrimitiveBinder registry (attach/detach/detachOwner/detachAll) that drives the host lifecycle over the EXISTING views PrimitiveBinding seam — attached(ctx) → wrap each tagged source in a PrimitiveBinding → register target:'pane' sources into the owner's PaneScene → schedule one Render frame; detach fires detached() EXACTLY ONCE (keyed on primitive identity) + unregisters, idempotent; (2) the PrimitiveContext factory ctxFor (chart/series/pane + requestUpdate→UpdateLevel + images→backend.createImage + lazy input) and placementFor (the owner's scale side for price-axis homing); (3) SERIES-attached: wrapping model Series.attachPrimitive/detachPrimitive in wireSeries (keeps the model #primitives push for autoscale, layers the host binding on top); auto-detach via destroySeries → binder.detachOwner; (4) PANE-attached: the pane handle's attachPrimitive/detachPrimitive (forward-declared hooks populated post-binder); (5) chart.dispose → binder.detachAll. Realized api ≈3443; 3500 leaves ~57 headroom. Total recomputed 20800→21050.
$rationale_extras_m13: 2026-06-15 (M13 — EXTENSIBILITY-SEAM PROOFS): extras raised 1800→3350 (§11 speed-bump). The architecture §11 ~1800 estimate covered the four M12 first-party plugins (series/up-down markers + text/image watermarks ≈1503 + shared adapter+barrel ≈129) but PRE-DATED the M13 in-tree seam proofs, which the milestone REQUIRES as real source (the V1-REQ T1–T8 / I1–I6 / L1–L6 / S1–S5 checklists are the tests, exercised against the PUBLIC api): tool-host.ts ≈505 (the design 05 §4 creation/edit FSM + ShapeHandle/ToolDefinition/Anchor/SerializedShape ownership + the in-tree trend-line ToolDefinition T7), ema.ts ≈253 (the §5 single-ingress indicator host + IndicatorDefinition/Computer/Output shapes + the streaming EMA recurrence), sync-group.ts ≈225 (the §6 N-chart range+crosshair mirror over the three loss-free public seams + echo suppression), and the §7 horizontal-scale behaviors price-axis-behavior.ts ≈146 (price + yield-curve, open-scale weight bands) / timezone-time-behavior.ts ≈221 (IANA→offset + the A-5 offset-aware wrapped time behavior) / session-highlight.ts ≈285 (the §7.1 keysInRange-driven BelowSeries band + time-axis-break dual-source primitive). Each carries the spec-of-record citation discipline (design 05 §4–§7 + study 03/08 references inline), which the §11 count includes by definition (comments+blanks counted). Realized extras ≈3362 after the adversarial-review fixes (EMA whitespace index-space, sync crosshair-echo + split guards, session full-span band, multi-anchor FSM, tool test rigor); raised again 3350→3400 (≈38 headroom). NO core rework — every line is under src/extras/ (the M13 zero-core-rework gate). Total recomputed 21050→22650.
$rationale_api_seamfix: 2026-06-15 (M13 follow-up — INCOMPLETE PUBLIC SEAMS): api raised 3500→3550 (§11 speed-bump). The M13 zero-core-rework milestone exposed three public seams whose real impls existed but whose FORWARDING was missing (a real v1 defect — the methods returned null/[]/stub). create-chart.ts's makeTimeScaleHandle (FIX 6) was a pure stub: coordinateToLogical/logicalToCoordinate/keyToLogical/logicalToKey/timeToCoordinate/timeToLogical/snapToBar→null, keysInRange→[], isEmpty→true. It is now wired over the shared Timeline (keyToLogical/logicalToKey/keysInRange/nearestSlotAt — all PUBLIC) + a chart-level HorzGeometry built on demand from the live { paneWidth, barSpacing, rightOffset, baseIndex } (mirroring wireSeries.rebuild), so a live chart's timeScale().coordinateToLogical(x) returns a real number after setData (proven by the new create-chart.test.ts conversions block). The wiring adds the RIGHT_AXIS_WIDTH constant (shared by the measure hook + the handle's pane-width reconstruction), the geom()/timeToLogical helpers, and the ~12 forwarded port methods (the setVisibleLogicalRange setter + coordinateToTime remain documented no-ops/null pending the model time-scale navigator, a separate seam). Realized api ≈3509; 3550 leaves ~41 headroom. NO core rework — FIX 4/5 (the timeBehavior({timezoneOffset}) injection + offset-aware fillWeights/formatTick) land in data (1164/1500) and the timezone-time-behavior A-5 collapse shrank extras (3362→3253). Total recomputed 22650→22700.
$rationale_host_interaction: 2026-06-15 (LIVE INTERACTION JOIN — Part A): host raised 2050→2310 (§11 speed-bump). The M8 §7 deliverable 'Pointer-Events handling' was specified but never wired — a rendered chart was inert to mouse/touch (only synthetic-pointer unit tests existed). Part A adds the missing DOM→GestureMachine feed: src/host/input/dom-input.ts (the §7 Pointer-Events + capture adapter — attachDomInput attaches pointerdown/move/up/cancel + wheel to one surface mount, setPointerCapture on down so a drag keeps delivering moves outside the element, translates each DOM PointerEvent → GesturePointer in SURFACE-LOCAL media px via a getContentOrigin getter, and routes wheel through normalizeWheel with preventDefault so the page does not scroll) ≈190 incl. the spec-of-record header + the narrowed DomInputTarget/PointerEventLike/WheelEventLike interfaces (kept headless-testable with synthetic events), and the chart-host.ts wiring (one adapter per interactive surface in #mkSurface feature-detected on addEventListener, the #onWheel method that builds the discrete 'wheel' GestureEvent and routes it through the SAME #onGesture sink the recognized gestures use, the #domUnsubs teardown in dispose, and the wheelSpeed/windowsChromium deps) ≈55. NO change to gestures/router/behaviors. Realized host ≈2262; 2310 leaves ~48 headroom. Total recomputed 22700→22960.
$rationale_api_interaction: 2026-06-15 (LIVE INTERACTION JOIN — Part B): api raised 3550→3760 (§11 speed-bump). Part B makes the host pan/zoom/reset/fit ports actually MOVE the chart (they were no-op markInput()+invalidate stubs) and the geometry read LIVE state (rebuild + makeTimeScaleHandle were HARDCODED barSpacing:6/rightOffset:0). create-chart.ts gains: createLiveNav — the chart's ONE live { barSpacing, rightOffset } cell whose pan/zoom/reset/fit mutators DELEGATE all scroll/scale math to the model time-scale navigator's pure functions (clampBarSpacing/clampRightOffset/rightOffsetForPixels/fitContentWithPixels — no nav math reinvented in api, the §11 boundary) with the clamp params read from the live timeScale options ≈150; the LiveNav interface; drivePriceAxisDrag (frames a start→scaleTo→end on the pane's price-scale navigator — the seam is driven, the visible price-axis effect bounded by the M12-deferred autoscale→navigator-range wiring, documented inline) ≈20; buildHost's four real hooks (pan→nav.pan, zoom→nav.zoom, resetPane→nav.reset, priceAxisDrag→drivePriceAxisDrag, each + invalidate Render) + the nav threading through wireSeries/rebuild + makeTimeScaleHandle (live barSpacing/rightOffset, fitContent→nav.fit, reset→nav.reset) ≈40. createLiveNav is exported (NOT re-exported from the api barrel) so the Part B nav unit tests drive the math directly. Realized api ≈3719; 3760 leaves ~41 headroom. NO core rework — the navigator math functions already existed in model. Total recomputed 22960→23170.
$rationale_api_yaxis: 2026-06-16 (Y-AXIS MOVEMENT — autoscale→navigator-range seam CLOSED): api raised 4200→4260 (§11 speed-bump). The price (y) axis was frozen: priceConverterFor derived the paint range from priceRangeOf(store) — the WHOLE-store min/max — so it never re-fit as you pan/zoom (the visible window was ignored) and never read the pane's PriceNavigator (a price-axis drag updated model state the paint never consumed; worse, the navigator's range started null so startScale() early-returned → the drag was a literal no-op). This is the seam the AXIS-FURNITURE + Part-B notes explicitly DEFERRED (create-chart.ts:969/:1536). The fix (study 04 §3.4/§4.8): visibleIndexWindow(store, horz) maps the live HorzGeometry's visible logical range to a clamped bar-index window; resolvePriceRange(store, scale, horz) returns the navigator's PINNED range when the scale is manually-scaled (autoScale off) else fits that VISIBLE window; priceGeometryParamsFor/priceConverterFor thread the one resolved range so the series rebuild AND buildFurnitureGeometry paint identical Ys (grid/labels stay pixel-aligned); buildFurnitureGeometry SEEDS the navigator each frame (setHeight=internal band for the §4.8 damping, setRange=fitted while autoscaling) so a drag has a valid snapshot; resetPane (double-click) re-enables autoScale + clears the pinned range. Realized api ≈4221; 4260 leaves ~39 headroom. Model +9 (navigator.setHeight + PriceScale.setHeight/setAutoScale passthroughs, well within 2973/4800). Total recomputed 23610→23670.
$rationale_api_axes: 2026-06-15 (AXIS FURNITURE JOIN): api raised 3760→4200 (§11 speed-bump). createChart rendered only the series candles — the M7 furniture sources (grid/last-value-label) + the model tick engines EXISTED but were never registered into the axis/pane scenes, because buildHost's paneConfigs/measure returned a FRESH newScene() each call (the host captures config.scene once at #buildTree, so anything registered into a throwaway scene was lost). The fix MEMOIZES the per-pane right/left price-axis scenes + the one bottom time-axis scene (like sceneFor) and installs furniture into the STABLE scenes once per pane (installFurniture, lazy on the first rightAxisSceneFor resolve): (1) GRID → the pane scene (band Grid, below the series) via the views createGridSource, fed the price-tick Ys (rebuildTickMarks over a PriceGeometry built from priceGeometryParamsFor — the SAME range/margins priceConverterFor gives the candles) + the time-tick Xs (the visible strict range thinned by the model TickMarkEngine over the timeline weights, mapped through the live HorzGeometry); (2) PRICE AXIS (right) → the right-axis scene: the price TICK LABELS (a thin api-side SceneSource emitting one media text command, right-aligned within RIGHT_AXIS_WIDTH=60, formatted by the fmt priceFormatter) + the last-value pill via the views createLastValueLabelSource at the series' last close; (3) TIME AXIS (bottom) → the time-axis scene: the time TICK LABELS (a thin api-side source, behavior.formatTick of the timeline.internalAt(slot) item, centered on each tick X within the 28px height). All furniture recomputes per frame from the pane's primary-series store + the live nav cell (buildFurnitureGeometry mirrors wireSeries.rebuild) so grid lines + labels align with the candles and track pan/zoom/fit on the SAME invalidate(Render) frames. The api-side emit is sanctioned (§3.1: api may import views/gfx/model/fmt + emit gfx via DisplayListBuilder). Realized api ≈4127; 4200 leaves ~73 headroom. data +0 budget (1172/1500): the one behavior-agnostic Timeline.internalAt(slot) accessor the time-label path needs (so a number-keyed behavior gets its number, the time behavior its {timestamp}) fits the existing data budget. Total recomputed 23170→23610.
